name: faster-whisper
description: Install Faster-Whisper, a local speech-to-text service for OpenEnsemble. Both profiles run large-v3-turbo (~810 MB on disk); the difference is runtime: 'cpu' uses int8 quantization (~2 GB RAM, speed varies by CPU — ~2-3× realtime on modern desktops, near-realtime on low-end), 'cuda' uses float16 (~2.5 GB VRAM, ~14-40× realtime, needs NVIDIA GPU + driver). Speaks the OpenAI /v1/audio/transcriptions API so it slots into OE's STT path with sttApiUrl=http://127.0.0.1:5154/v1/audio/transcriptions. Re-running is safe — existing venv/model/service are detected and skipped. Pass profile='cuda' in the recipe args to install the GPU build (else CPU).

profile.description: cpu = distil-large-v3 int8 (no GPU). cuda = large-v3-turbo float16 (NVIDIA GPU required).
